name: Ramesses II
description: Ramesses II, also known as Ramesses the Great, reigned as the third pharaoh of the Nineteenth Dynasty of Egypt during the New Kingdom. Considered one of the most powerful and celebrated pharaohs in Egyptian history, he ruled for an impressive 66 years, leaving an indelible mark on the land. Ramesses II is renowned for his extensive building programs, including the construction of numerous temples, monuments, and cities. He commissioned the magnificent Abu Simbel temples, dedicated to himself and his queen Nefertari, and expanded the Karnak Temple complex, adding the Great Hypostyle Hall. His military prowess was demonstrated in the Battle of Kadesh against the Hittite Empire, a pivotal conflict that ultimately led to one of the earliest known peace treaties in history. Ramesses II also fathered a vast number of children, solidifying his dynasty and ensuring its continuation. He is often seen as the epitome of a pharaoh: a divine ruler, a skilled warrior, and a prolific builder, whose reign ushered in an era of prosperity and stability for Egypt. His legacy continues to captivate and inspire, making him a central figure in Egyptian history and mythology, showcasing the power and grandeur of ancient Egypt at its zenith. He is also known for his diplomatic skills, forging alliances and maintaining peace with neighboring kingdoms, securing Egypt's borders and trade routes. His impact on Egyptian art, architecture, and culture is undeniable, solidifying his place as one of the greatest pharaohs of all time.
subtitle: The Great Builder, Warrior, Pharaoh
achievements: Fought in the Battle of Kadesh
Oversaw a period of great prosperity

keyMyths.title: Battle of Kadesh
keyMyths.description: Ramesses II led the Egyptian army against the Hittite Empire at Kadesh. Although a strategic draw, the battle solidified Ramesses' image as a powerful warrior and leader.
keyMyths.source: Various historical texts and temple inscriptions

keyMyths.title: Construction of Abu Simbel
keyMyths.description: Ramesses II commissioned the construction of the Abu Simbel temples to honor himself and his queen Nefertari. The temples are a testament to his power and architectural ambition.

keyMyths.title: The First Peace Treaty
keyMyths.description: After years of conflict, Ramesses II signed a peace treaty with the Hittite king Hattusili III. This treaty is one of the oldest known peace treaties in history.

sources.author: Christian Jacq
sources.description: Fictionalized account of Ramesses II's life, blending history with adventure.
sources.text: Fictionalized account of Ramesses II's life, blending history with adventure.
